task_summary: The ai-timeline-agent needs hardening in the fallback path that parses assistant text into tool calls: today it can execute inline `create_task({...})` text that appears in prose/examples instead of as an actual tool call. Attachment-preserving image request handling is largely already wired through transport, prompting, and `executeCreateTask`; the highest-value remaining work is to tighten parser/cleanup behavior and add regression tests around the April 9 z-image orbital-view case and selected-image follow-ups like `of it` and `without style`.

key_evidence.point: `extractToolCalls` first uses structured tool calls, but if none are present it falls back to regex parsing over assistant text and accepts any inline `create_task({...})` match.

The parsed fallback tool calls are executed immediately in the main loop, and `cleanAssistantText` separately strips any `create_task(...)` text from assistant output, so a false positive can both run unintended work and hide the evidence in the transcript.

Attachment transport/persistence is already end-to-end: the client sends `selected_clips`, the edge function normalizes/enriches them, and the loop reuses the most recent stored attachments when a follow-up message arrives without reattaching media.

The system prompt already contains explicit guidance that selected-image requests like `of it` / `without style` should stay on the selected-image path and prefer `image-to-image` rather than style transfer or plain text-to-image.

`executeCreateTask` already consumes selected reference URLs to derive `selectedReferenceClips`, reuse shot context, and auto-fill `based_on`; there is already a z-image `image-to-image` regression asserting this path with the orbital-view wording.
Existing tests cover positive extraction of text-formatted `create_task` calls and the orbital prompt, but there is no negative regression proving quoted/example `create_task(...)` text is ignored.

relevant_code.why: Primary fallback parser for assistant-text tool calls; most likely root cause for fake `create_task` execution.
Consumes extracted tool calls, cleans assistant text, and preserves/reuses attachments across turns.
System-prompt routing guidance for style-vs-selected-image requests already contains the `of it` / `without style` language; useful to verify rather than re-implement blindly.
Actual selected-image task creation path; verifies whether attachment-preserving edits need code changes or only routing/test changes.
Entry point where incoming `selected_clips` are normalized and enriched before the loop runs.
Normalizes inbound attachment payloads and backfills prompt metadata from generations.
Client-side serialization of composer attachments into the `selected_clips` API payload.

test_expectations.what_it_checks: Quoted/example assistant prose containing `create_task({...})` or orbital-view sample text does not produce an executable tool call.
Assistant text cleanup removes real tool-call syntax but preserves explanatory/example `create_task(...)` text that was not executed.
Real text-formatted `Tool call create_task:` blocks for the z-image orbital-view prompt still parse successfully.
System prompt keeps explicit routing guidance for selected-image requests like `of it` / `without style` toward `image-to-image`.
Selected-image `image-to-image` requests keep the attachment URL, shot reuse, and `based_on` generation on the z-image path.
Client attachment serialization still includes generation and shot metadata needed for follow-up requests.

constraints: Do not break the structured `tool_calls` path or the existing positive fallback formats (`Tool call create_task:` blocks and any intentionally supported standalone invocation syntax).

Do not regress the selected-clip pipeline: 1. `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.ts:271-288`, 2. `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/index.ts:28-69`, 3. `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/selectedClips.ts:32-124`, 4. `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/loop.ts:390-400`, 5. `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.ts:206-263`.
Do not remove or weaken the prompt guidance that keeps `of it` / `without style` requests on an attachment-aware image path.
Any parser change must stay aligned with transcript cleanup so the agent does not execute or silently erase non-executed example text.
suggested_approach: Treat the parser as the primary fix site, not the prompt. The obvious prompt-level routing work is already present in `prompts.ts:165-170`, and selected-clip plumbing is already in place. Audit the parser/cleanup call chain in order: 1. `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tool-calls.ts:104-234`, 2. `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/loop.ts:368-371`, 3. `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/loop.ts:508-531`. Tighten fallback parsing so `create_task` is only accepted when it is clearly emitted as a tool call (explicit `Tool call ...` block or other intentionally supported standalone form), not when it appears inside prose, quoted examples, or explanatory text. Then mirror that rule in `cleanAssistantText`, and add regressions around the April 9 orbital-view wording plus attachment-aware follow-ups so the agent still honors real selected-image edits while ignoring fake tool text.
